en.summary: This prophetic tradition, transmitted through a Basran chain via the prominent companion Anas ibn Malik, presents a concise maxim contrasting two moral qualities. The Prophet declares that vulgarity (fuhsh) invariably degrades whatever it touches, while modesty (haya') invariably beautifies it. The hadith was likely addressed to a general audience as moral instruction, using the rhetorical device of parallel antithesis to make the teaching memorable. In early Islamic society, haya' encompassed both inner bashfulness before God and outward decorum in speech and conduct.
en.seo_question: What did the Prophet Muhammad say about vulgarity and modesty?

ur.summary: یہ نبوی حدیث انس بن مالک کے ذریعے بصری سند سے منقول ہے۔ آنحضرت نے دو اخلاقی صفات کا مقابلہ کیا: بدزبانی ہر چیز کو خراب کرتی ہے، جبکہ حیاء ہر چیز کو خوبصورت بناتی ہے۔
ur.seo_question: نبی کریم نے بدزبانی اور حیاء کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟

tr.summary: Bu nebevî hadis, Basralı sahabi Enes bin Mâlik kanalıyla aktarılmıştır. Hz. Peygamber, çirkinliğin her şeyi bozduğunu, hayânın ise her şeyi güzelleştirdiğini birçok antitez kullanarak öğretmiştir.
tr.seo_question: Hz. Muhammed çirkinlik ve hayâ hakkında ne buyurmuştur?

fa.summary: این حدیث نبوی از طریق انس بن مالک با سند بصری نقل شده است. پیامبر با استفاده از صنعت مقابله، زشتی و حیاء را در برابر هم قرار داده است.
fa.seo_question: پیامبر درباره زشتی و حیاء چه فرمود؟

id.summary: Hadis nabawi ini diriwayatkan melalui jalur Bashrah dari sahabat Anas bin Malik. Nabi mengontraskan dua sifat moral: kekejian yang merusak dan rasa malu yang memperindah segala sesuatu.
id.seo_question: Apa yang disabdakan Nabi Muhammad tentang kekejian dan rasa malu?

bn.summary: এই নবী হাদীসটি সাহাবী আনাস বিন মালিকের মাধ্যমে বর্ণিত। নবী অশ্লীলতা ও লজ্জার তুলনা করেছেন।
bn.seo_question: নবী মুহাম্মদ অশ্লীলতা ও লজ্জা সম্পর্কে কী বলেছেন?

es.summary: Esta tradición profética se transmite a través del compañero Anas ibn Mālik por una cadena basora. El Profeta contrasta la vulgaridad, que siempre degrada, con la modestia, que siempre embellece.
es.seo_question: ¿Qué dijo el Profeta Mahoma sobre la vulgaridad y la modestia?

fr.summary: Cette tradition prophétique est transmise par le compagnon Anas ibn Mālik via une chaîne basrienne. Le Prophète oppose la vulgarité, qui dégrade toujours, à la pudeur, qui embellit toujours.
fr.seo_question: Qu’a dit le Prophète Muḥammad sur la vulgarité et la pudeur ?

de.summary: Diese prophetische Überlieferung wird über den Gefährten Anas ibn Mālik durch eine basrische Kette überliefert. Der Prophet stellt Vulgarität, die stets beschädigt, der Bescheidenheit gegenüber, die stets verschönert.
de.seo_question: Was sagte der Prophet Muhammad über Vulgarität und Bescheidenheit?

ru.summary: Этот пророческий хадис передан через сподвижника Анаса ибн Малика по басрийской цепочке. Пророк противопоставляет непристойность, которая портит, стыдливости, которая украшает.
ru.seo_question: Что сказал Пророк Мухаммад о непристойности и стыдливости?

zh.summary: 这条先知圣训通过圣伴艾奈斯·本·马利克的巴士拉传述链传达。先知对比了粗俗和廉耻两种品德：前者必然败坏事物，后者必然美化事物。
zh.seo_question: 先知穆罕默德对粗俗和廉耻说了什么？

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِمْرَانَ الْمَرْزُبَانِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ الْحَكِيمِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ مَعِينٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا مَعْمَرٌ عَنْ ثَابِتٍ عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: مَا كَانَ الْفُحْشُ فِي شَيْءٍ قَطُّ إِلَّا شَانَهُ وَلَا كَانَ الْحَيَاءُ فِي شَيْءٍ قَطُّ إِلَّا زَانَهُ

narrators.name_ar: أَبُو عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِمْرَانَ الْمَرْزُبَانِيُّ
narrators.name_en: Abu Ubaydillah Muhammad ibn Imran al-Marzbani
narrators.known_identity: Abu Ubaydillah Muhammad ibn Imran al-Marzbani (d. 384 AH), literary scholar and hadith transmitter in Baghdad
Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Hakimi, hadith transmitter

narrators.ambiguity_note: Common name among hadith transmitters; likely Muhammad ibn Ishaq al-Saghani based on the chain context

narrators.known_identity: Yahya ibn Ma'in (d. 233 AH), renowned hadith critic and master of narrator evaluation (jarh wa ta'dil)
Abd al-Razzaq ibn Hammam al-San'ani (d. 211 AH), compiler of al-Musannaf, major hadith authority
Ma'mar ibn Rashid (d. 153 AH), Basran hadith scholar, teacher of Abd al-Razzaq
Thabit ibn Aslam al-Bunani (d. 127 AH), prominent Basran tabi'i known for narrating from Anas ibn Malik
Anas ibn Malik (d. ~93 AH), companion of the Prophet who served him for ten years in Medina

key_phrases.phrase_en: May God bless him and his family and grant them peace

similar_content_hints.description: Hadith narrated in various collections stating that modesty (haya') is a branch of faith (iman), emphasizing the integral connection between faith and bashfulness.
Prophetic narrations on the virtue of good character (husn al-khuluq) and that the best of people are those with the finest manners, contrasting refined speech with vulgar speech.
Narrations from Imam Ali (AS) and other Imams warning against obscene speech (fuhsh) and its corrupting effect on social bonds and personal reputation.
